page_content:
CONS670 Conservation Biology (3 Credits)

Conservation in the Anthropocene means conserving biodiversity

and ecosystem function in the midst of climate change, habitat loss, overexploitation, altered nutrient cycling, and invasive species with protected areas and reserve networks, ecosystem restoration, and other biodiversity conservation and management schemes.Cross-listed with MEES670.

Credit Only Granted for. CONS670 or MEES670.

CONS680 Problem Solving in Conservation/Development (4 Credits) Students will be exposed to current problems in conservation and development through great lectures, field trips, interviews and appropriate literature. Working in teams, students will formulate recommendations based on a synthesis of biological, economic and policy considerations.
